creator: emz@norma.perm.ru
text:
www/chromium often shows corrupted pages, like here: http://static.enaza.ru/userupload/gyazo/3440665460a77084c77cf02d68ae.png
This started about some time ago (may be about a year). First I thought that this is a problem with dependencies/memory/something else. But since that I ruled out all of these problems. Plus, this happens only on first opening of the page - when F5 is pressed, the page reloads and displays just fine (often F5 need to be hit multiple times).

I'm using the 

chromium-38.0.2125.101_2

on a 

FreeBSD bsdrookie.norma.com. 10.1-BETA1 FreeBSD 10.1-BETA1 #0 r271608: Mon Sep 15 14:14:36 YEKT 2014     emz@bsdrookie.norma.com.:/usr/obj/usr/src/sys/GENERIC  amd64

but this isn't really the version. I've seen such artifacts from the chromium 33.x at least. 

I'm using Firefox as my primary browser, and everything is fine in it.
